page.title: vitiligo — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for vitiligo: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for vitiligo: 108 alterations and 88 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3 provides benefits in vitiligo.
Curcumin and capsaicin might represent an alternative approach to protect against vitiligo progression.
Effectiveness of topical Nigella sativa for vitiligo treatment.
Efficacy and safety of ruxolitinib cream combined with narrow-band UVB phototherapy for treatment of vitiligo.
Gingko biloba is effective in slowing the progression of vitiligo.
Ginkgo biloba halts the progression of vitilgo, and may stimulate repigmentation in some patients.
Glycyrrhizin and ultraviolet B light therapy improved active-stage generalized vitiligo.
Homeopathy may be effective in the early stages of vitiligo.
Long-term efficacy and safety of oral abrocitinib combined with narrow-band UVB phototherapy in non-segmental vitiligo.
Low-dose baricitinib plus narrow-band ultraviolet B for the treatment of progressive non-segmental vitiligo.
Narrow-band ultraviolet B is a useful and well-tolerated treatment for vitiligo.
NB-UVB plus oral Polypodium leucotomos extract display higher efficacy than NB-UVB alone in patients with vitiligo.
No association was observed between the number of NB-UVB treatments and carcinogenesis in any study group.
Once-weekly targeted ultraviolet B phototherapy appears to be as efficacious as the twice-weekly regimen in vitiligo.
Simultaneous use of narrowband ultraviolet B and topical piperine has a remarkable effect on treatment of vitiligo.
The effect of vitamin C on oxidative stress indices and skin regimentation of vitiligo patients.
The efficacy of narrowband ultraviolet B phototherapy combination with tofacitinib in the treatment of vitiligo.
This study showed that probably Silybum marianum is a good choice for patients with vitiligo.
Topical epigallocatechin-3-gallate in the treatment of vitiligo.
Treatment outcome and persistence of repigmentation from narrow-band ultraviolet B phototherapy in vitiligo.
Turmeric cream can be used as an alternative remedy or adjuvant therapy in mild to moderate vitiligo lesions.
UVB is an effective modality for treatment of vitiligo.
UVB-IPL phototherapy seems to be effective and well-tolerated in non-segmental vitiligo.
Vitamin B12, folic acid and sunlight appear an effective therapy in Vitiligo.
Vitiligo treated with combined piperine-based topical treatment and narrowband ultraviolet B therapy
Apigenin protects human melanocytes against oxidative damage by activation of the Nrf2 pathway.
Harmine and Kaempferol treatment enhances NFATC1 and FOXP3 mediated regulatory T-cells' suppressive capacity in generalized vitiligo.
These results provide new evidence for the effectiveness of EGCG in vitiligo treatment.
Galangin alleviates vitiligo by targeting ANXA2 degradation in macrophages.
Isorhamnetin protects melanocytes against CD8+ T cell-mediated apoptosis and reduces inflammation in vitiligo-afflicted mouse.
Mechanisms of action of Lycium barbarum polysaccharide in protecting against vitiligo.
New insights in vitiligo treatments using bioactive compounds from Piper nigrum.
Novel berberine-loaded hyalurosomes as a promising nanodermatological treatment for vitiligo.
Preliminary testing of cancer vaccines caused mice to develop vitiligo.
Seed powder of Psoralea corylifolia could be an effective monotherapy for small circular white lesions of vitiligo.
Tanshinone IIA alleviates vitiligo by suppressing AKT mediated CD8 + T cells activation.
Topical treatment with piperine improves the treatment response to UV irradiation in the mouse model of Vitiligo.
6-shogaol protects human melanocytes against oxidative stress through activation of the Nrf2-antioxidant response element signalling pathway.
A combination of green tea, quercetin and folate may contribute to reducing melanocyte oxidative stress in Vitiligo.
A review of the skin regenerative potentials of Curcumin.

natural_products.name: 3,5,7-trihydroxy-2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromone
natural_products.key_findings: Human In Vitro: Harmine and Kaempferol treatment enhances NFATC1 and FOXP3 mediated regulatory T-cells' suppressive capacity in generalized vitiligo.

natural_products.name: Goji
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study: Mechanisms of action of Lycium barbarum polysaccharide in protecting against vitiligo.

natural_products.name: Babchi
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study, In Vitro Study: Seed powder of Psoralea corylifolia could be an effective monotherapy for small circular white lesions of vitiligo.

natural_products.name: Tanshinone IIA
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study: Tanshinone IIA alleviates vitiligo by suppressing AKT mediated CD8 + T cells activation.

natural_products.name: 6-Shogaol
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: 6-shogaol protects human melanocytes against oxidative stress through activation of the Nrf2-antioxidant response element signalling pathway.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
